channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Police shooting suspect Desmond Freeman shot dead after seven-month manhunt in Victoria
item.description: After a seven-month search, alleged police killer Desmond ‘Dezi’ Freeman has been shot dead by heavily armed officers in Victoria’s north-east. Freeman was accused of murdering two police officers and...

item.title: Vedanta seeks Supreme Court intervention against Adani’s approved acquisition of Jaypee assets
item.description: Mining conglomerate Vedanta has approached the Supreme Court of India seeking a stay on the acquisition of the insolvent Jaypee Group’s assets by Gautam Adani’s Adani Group. The legal action follows t...

item.title: Grasslands National Park restricts access to protect endangered bird habitat
item.description: Grasslands National Park in Saskatchewan has implemented temporary access restrictions to a section of its protected area in order to safeguard an endangered bird species. Park officials emphasize tha...

item.title: Ramaphosa Sets Up Task Team to Cushion South Africans From April Fuel and Food Price Hikes
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has announced the formation of a ministerial task team to find ways of protecting South Africans from the steep fuel price increase expected in April. The announcement came d...

item.title: Economic Pressures Mount as Iran Conflict Continues Without Diplomatic Solutions
item.description: On the 31st day of the US-Israeli conflict with Iran, the economic consequences of the war are becoming more severe. Military operations remain intense across multiple fronts, including Iran, Israel, ...

item.title: Jocelyn Peterman joins Team Einarson after lineup overhaul
item.description: Team Einarson, a prominent curling rink from Gimli, Manitoba, has announced the addition of two-time Olympian Jocelyn Peterman as lead and vice, following a significant restructuring of their roster. ...

item.title: UN formally recognises transatlantic slavery as a crime against humanity
item.description: On 25 March 2026, the United Nations General Assembly adopted Resolution A/80/L.48, officially recognising the trafficking of Africans and the system of racialised chattel slavery as the gravest crime...

item.title: Nigerian Banks Record 96% Compliance as CBN’s Recapitalisation Programme Nears Completion
item.description: The Association of Corporate Affairs Managers of Banks (ACAMB) don hail the Nigerian banking sector for showing strong capacity and discipline in meeting the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN)’s recapitali...

item.title: Prominent Islamabad businessman dies in armed robbery at farmhouse
item.description: In Islamabad, a well-known businessman, Amir Awan, tragically lost his life after being shot by armed robbers during a dacoity at his farmhouse in the Orchard Scheme area early on Monday morning. Amir...

item.title: Kenyan Youth and Marginalised Communities Encouraged to Register as Voters for Greater Political Voice
item.description: In Kenya, the Political Parties Liaison Committee (PPLC) Chairman Evans James Misati has called on young people and members of marginalised and minority groups to actively take part in the ongoing enh...

item.title: PMD forecasts widespread rainfall and thunderstorms across Pakistan from April 1 to April 4
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a forecast for widespread rainfall and thunderstorms across different regions of the country starting from April 1, Wednesday, continuing until ...

item.title: Trump’s Iran Gamble: A Lesson in Misguided Deal-Making
item.description: This article from The Economist analyses how President Donald Trump’s handling of the Iran crisis reflects a contradiction between his own past business advice and his current diplomatic approach. Dra...

item.title: High Road Traffic Expected Over Easter Bank Holiday Amid Increased Domestic Travel
item.description: This Easter bank holiday weekend is projected to witness the busiest road traffic since 2022, with nearly 21 million trips planned across the UK. Research by the RAC and traffic information provider I...

item.title: BJP nominates Harshad Parmar for Umreth Assembly bypoll following former MLA’s demise
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has announced Mr. Harshad Parmar as its candidate for the upcoming by-election to the Umreth Assembly constituency in Gujarat. This bypoll has arisen due to the unfort...

item.title: Scientific Review of Peptides for Exercise Performance and Recovery
item.description: Peptides, which are short chains of amino acids, are increasingly promoted as supplements that can enhance workout performance, support muscle growth, and aid recovery. Unlike whole proteins like whey...

item.title: Yas Launches New Outlet at Dodoma SGR Station to Improve Digital Service Access
item.description: Dodoma residents and passengers using the Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) are set to enjoy easier access to communication and financial services with the opening of a new Yas store at the SGR station. Th...

item.title: China Successfully Launches Lijian 2 Rocket Carrying Qingzhou 1 Cargo Vehicle
item.description: On March 30, 2026, at 11:00 UTC, the first Lijian 2 rocket (also known as Kinetica 2) successfully launched from the Dongfeng Commercial Space Center's LC-40 pad in Jiuquan, Inner Mongolia. Developed ...

item.title: Intelligence agencies expose alleged Indian plan to stage border incident using Pakistani prisoners
item.description: According to Pakistani security sources, intelligence agencies have uncovered a purported plan by India to carry out a false flag operation in Indian Illegally Occupied Jammu and Kashmir (IIOJK). The ...

item.title: Professor Olonisakin Calls for Increased Investment in Medicinal Plant Research and Laboratory Infrastructure
item.description: Professor Adebisi Olonisakin, organic chemist for Adekunle Ajasin University, Akungba Akoko, don call on Nigerian government and stakeholders make dem put more money and effort into research on plant ...

item.title: Tanganyika Law Society conference to discuss role in achieving National Vision 2050
item.description: As Tanzania prepares for key development goals under the National Vision 2050, the Tanganyika Law Society (TLS) is arranging its annual conference at the Arusha International Conference Centre from 27...
